inclusion_criteria: Randomized controlled trials (parallel or randomized crossover) with an exercise-matched comparator following an unrestricted eating window; pre- and post-intervention data available
Adults (≥18 years), generally healthy (may include overweight/obesity), undertaking structured aerobic, resistance, or concurrent training during the intervention
Intervention: time-restricted eating with a daily feeding window of 4–12 hours combined with exercise; intervention duration ≥4 weeks
Reports body composition outcomes: fat mass (kg) and/or body fat percentage and/or fat-free mass (kg), measured with validated methods
exclusion_criteria: Non-randomized, uncontrolled, observational, or acute studies; intervention duration <4 weeks; no pre–post outcome data
Ineligible population: <18 years, pregnant/lactating, or clinical populations with chronic diseases/medications likely to affect metabolism/body composition (beyond overweight/obesity)

Ineligible intervention/control: no exercise component in one or both groups, not exercise-matched, control uses a restricted eating window, or groups differ in prescribed energy/macronutrient intake beyond eating-window timing
Does not report at least one target body composition outcome (FM kg, BF%, or FFM kg)
screening_criteria.description: Screen studies evaluating time-restricted eating (4–12 h feeding window) combined with exercise versus an exercise-matched, unrestricted eating window control in adults, with body composition outcomes.

included_articles.title: Time-restricted feeding plus resistance training in active females: a randomized trial.
Effect of Time-Restricted Eating and Resistance Training on High-Speed Strength and Body Composition.
Hypercaloric 16:8 time-restricted eating during 8 weeks of resistance exercise in well-trained men and women.
Effects of eight weeks of time-restricted feeding (16/8) on basal metabolism, maximal strength, body composition, inflammation, and cardiovascular risk factors in resistance-trained males.
Effects of Time-Restricted Eating on Aerobic Capacity, Body Composition, and Markers of Metabolic Health in Healthy Male Recreational Runners: A Randomized Crossover Trial.
Four Weeks of 16/8 Time Restrictive Feeding in Endurance Trained Male Runners Decreases Fat Mass, without Affecting Exercise Performance.
Effects of 8 wk of 16:8 Time-restricted Eating in Male Middle- and Long-Distance Runners.
Time-restricted eating effects on performance, immune function, and body composition in elite cyclists: a randomized controlled trial.
Efficacy of time restricted eating and resistance training on body composition and mood profiles among young adults with overweight/obesity: a randomized controlled trial.
High-Protein Time-Restricted Eating Alongside Resistance Training Reduces Adipose Tissue While Preserving Fat-Free Mass in Women With Overweight: A Randomized Controlled Trial.
Flexible time-restricted eating combined with exercise in a free-living setting for middle-aged women with overweight/obesity: a randomized controlled trial.
Impact of 16/8 time-restricted eating on body composition and lipolytic hormone regulation in female DanceSport dancers.
